hook: You already know how to solve equations when everything is laid out neatly with numbers and symbols. But most real-world problems arrive as messy sentences, patterns, or puzzles — not ready-made equations. This unit gives you a toolkit of strategies so that any problem, no matter how it's disguised, becomes something you can crack.
concept: Every topic here is about translating the unknown into the familiar — turning confusing situations into organized math you can work with, a process sometimes called 'mathematical problem-solving strategy.'

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to convert phrases like 'five more than twice a number' into algebraic equations such as $2x + 5$ that you can solve step by step.
Master a structured trial-and-error approach where each guess gives you information that steers your next attempt closer to the correct answer.
Apply visual tools — number lines, bar models, and sketches — to make the relationships hidden inside a word problem immediately clear and solvable.
You will learn to identify repeating sequences and rules in numbers or shapes so you can predict any term in a pattern without listing every step.
Master the strategy of starting from a known result and reversing each operation to uncover the original unknown, especially useful when the end of a problem is given.
Apply tables and coordinate graphs to organize data, spot trends, and read off solutions in problems that involve two related quantities changing together.
